en: Chapter 1
zh: 第 1 章

en: Introduction To Flying
zh: 飞行入门

en: Introduction
zh: 引言

en: The Pilot's Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge provides basic knowledge for the student pilot learning to fly, as well as pilots seeking advanced pilot certification. For detailed information on a variety of specialized flight topics, see specific Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) handbooks and Advisory Circulars (ACs).
zh: 《飞行员航空知识手册》为正在学习飞行的学员飞行员以及寻求高级飞行认证的飞行员提供了基础知识。有关各种专业飞行主题的详细信息，请参阅美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）发布的相应手册和咨询通告（Advisory Circulates, ACs）。

en: This chapter offers a brief history of flight, introduces the history and role of the FAA in civil aviation, FAA regulations and standards, government references and publications, eligibility for pilot certificates, available routes to flight instruction, the role of the Certified Flight Instructor (CFI) and Designated Pilot Examiner (DPE) in flight training, Practical Test Standards (PTS), and new, industry-developed Airman Certification Standards (ACS) framework that will eventually replace the PTS.
zh: 本章简要介绍了飞行的发展历史，阐述了美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）在民用航空领域的作用及其相关法规与标准，以及政府发布的参考文件和出版物。同时，内容还涵盖了获取飞行员执照的资格要求、可选择的飞行培训课程、认证飞行教员（CFI）与指定飞行员考官（DPE）在飞行训练中的重要作用，实用考试标准（PTS），以及全新的、由行业主导制定的"飞行员认证标准"（ACS）框架——该框架最终将取代现有的PTS。

en: History of Flight
zh: 飞行历史

en: From prehistoric times, humans have watched the flight of birds, and longed to imitate them, but lacked the power to do so. Logic dictated that if the small muscles of birds can lift them into the air and sustain them, then the larger muscles of humans should be able to duplicate the feat. No one knew about the intricate mesh of muscles, sinew, heart, breathing system, and devices not unlike wing flaps, variable-camber and spoilers of the modern airplane that enabled a bird to fly. Still, thousands of years and countless lives were lost in attempts to fly like birds.
zh: 从史前时代起，人类就观察着鸟类的飞行，并渴望能够模仿它们，但却缺乏实现这一目标的能力。逻辑告诉我们：如果鸟类那些微小的肌肉能够将它们托举到空中并维持其飞行状态，那么人类更强壮的肌肉也应该能够完成同样的壮举。然而，没有人知道鸟类体内存在着如此复杂的肌肉网络、肌腱系统、心脏结构以及呼吸系统；此外，还有一些类似于现代飞机机翼襟翼、可变翼型装置等结构，这些因素共同使得鸟类得以飞翔。尽管如此，在尝试像鸟类一样飞行的过程中，人类还是付出了数千年的努力和无数生命的代价。

en: The identity of the first "bird-men" who fitted themselves with wings and leapt off of cliffs in an effort to fly are lost in time, but each failure gave those who wished to fly questions that needed to be answered. Where had the wing flappers gone wrong? Philosophers, scientists, and inventors offered solutions, but no one could add wings to the human body and soar like a bird. During the 1500s, Leonardo da Vinci filled pages of his notebooks with sketches of proposed flying machines, but most of his ideas were flawed because he clung to the idea of birdlike wings. [Figure 1-1] By 1655, mathematician, physicist, and inventor Robert Hooke concluded that the human body does not possess the strength to power artificial wings. He believed human flight would require some form of artificial propulsion.
zh: 那些最早尝试为自己装上翅膀、从悬崖上跃下试图飞翔的"鸟人"的身份，早已随着时间的流逝而消失在历史的长河中。然而，每一次失败的尝试都引发了那些渴望飞翔的人们的疑问：究竟是哪里出了问题呢？哲学家、科学家和发明家们提出了各种解决方案，但始终没有人能够真正将翅膀安装在人类的身体上，让人类像鸟类一样翱翔于天空之中。16世纪时，莱昂纳多·达·芬奇在他的笔记中画满了各种飞行机器的草图，但他的大部分想法都存在缺陷——因为他仍然固执地坚持使用类似鸟类的翅膀作为实现飞翔的手段。[图1-1]到1655年，数学家、物理学家兼发明家罗伯特·胡克得出了结论：人类的身体并不具备驱动人造翅膀所需的足够力量；他认为人类要想真正飞翔，就必须依靠某种形式的人工推进系统。

en: The quest for human flight led some practitioners in another direction. In 1783, the first manned hot air balloon, crafted by Joseph and Etienne Montgolfier, flew for 23 minutes. Ten days later, Professor Jacques Charles flew the first gas balloon. A madness for balloon flight captivated the public's imagination and for a time flying enthusiasts turned their expertise to the promise of lighter-than-air flight. But for all its majesty in the air, the balloon was little more than a billowing heap of cloth capable of no more than a one-way, downwind journey.
zh: 对人类实现飞行的探索促使一些研究者转向了另一个方向。1783年，由约瑟夫（Joseph）和艾蒂安·蒙戈尔菲埃（Etienne Montgolfier）制造的第一艘载人热气球飞行了23分钟。十天后，雅克·夏尔（Jacques Charles）教授驾驶了第一艘气体气球。这种对热气球飞行的狂热彻底吸引了公众的想象力；一时间，热衷于飞行的人们将他们的专业知识投入到实现"比空气更轻"的飞行目标上。然而，尽管热气球在空中展现出了令人惊叹的壮观景象，实际上它不过是一种蓬松的布料，只能顺着风向向下"旅行"（即不断向下坠落）。

en: Balloons solved the problem of lift, but that was only one of the problems of human flight. The ability to control speed and direction eluded balloonists. The solution to that problem lay in a child's toy familiar to the East for 2,000 years, but not introduced to the West until the 13th century—the kite. The kites used by the Chinese for aerial observation, to test winds for sailing, as a signaling device, and as a toy, held many of the answers to lifting a heavier-than-air device into the air.
zh: 气球解决了升力问题，但这只是人类飞行技术所面临的问题之一。控制气球的速度和方向对气球驾驶员来说仍然是个难题。这个问题的解决之道其实隐藏在一种儿童玩具中——这种玩具在东方已有2000年的使用历史，但直到13世纪才传入西方，那就是风筝。中国人使用风筝进行空中观测、判断航行所需的风向、传递信号，同时也把它当作玩具；而风筝本身就蕴含了许多关于如何让比空气重的物体升空的原理。

en: One of the men who believed the study of kites unlocked the secrets of winged flight was Sir George Cayley. Born in England 10 years before the Mongolfier balloon flight, Cayley spent his 84 years seeking to develop a heavier-than-air vehicle supported by kite-shaped wings. [Figure 1-2] The "Father of Aerial Navigation," Cayley discovered the basic principles on which the modern science of aeronautics is founded; built what is recognized as the first successful flying model; and tested the first full-size man-carrying airplane.
zh: 相信通过研究风筝能够揭开飞行秘密的人之一，是乔治·凯利爵士（Sir George Cayley）。他出生于英格兰，在蒙戈尔菲埃热气球首次升空的十年前。凯利花费了84年的时间致力于研发一种依靠风筝形状的翼片来支撑重于空气的飞行器。[图1-2]作为"航空导航之父"，凯利发现了现代航空科学所基于的基本原理；他制造出了被认为是世界上第一个成功的飞行模型，并测试了第一架能够载人的全尺寸飞机。

en: For the half-century after Cayley's death, countless scientists, flying enthusiasts, and inventors worked toward building a powered flying machine. Men, such as William Samuel Henson, who designed a huge monoplane that was propelled by a steam engine housed inside the fuselage, and Otto Lilienthal, who proved human flight in aircraft heavier than air was practical, worked toward the dream of powered flight. A dream turned into reality by Wilbur and Orville Wright at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, on December 17, 1903.
zh: 在凯利去世后的半个世纪里，无数科学家、飞行爱好者和发明家都在致力于制造能够自主飞行的机器。例如威廉·塞缪尔·亨森（William Samuel Henson）设计了一架巨大的单翼飞机，该飞机由安装在机身内部的蒸汽引擎驱动；奥托·李林塔尔（Otto Lilienthal）则证明了人类驾驶比空气更重的飞行器进行飞行的可行性。这一梦想最终在1903年12月17日由威尔伯和奥维尔·莱特（Wilbur and Orville Wright）在北卡罗来纳州的基蒂霍克（Kitty Hawk）实现了一一他们成功让飞机首次实现了动力飞行。

en: The bicycle-building Wright brothers of Dayton, Ohio, had experimented for 4 years with kites, their own homemade wind tunnel, and different engines to power their biplane. One of their great achievements in flight was proving the value of the scientific, rather than a build-it-and-see approach. Their biplane, The Flyer, combined inspired design and engineering with superior craftsmanship.
zh: 来自俄亥俄州代顿市的自行车制造世家莱特兄弟，花了四年时间研究风筝、他们自己制作的风洞模型，以及各种可用于驱动双翼飞机的发动机。他们在飞行技术方面的一个重大成就在于：证明了科学方法的重要性——而非仅仅依靠"动手试做"来解决问题的方式。他们的双翼飞机"飞行者号"（The Flyer）将富有创意的设计理念与精湛的工程技术完美结合在一起。

en: Figure 1-3. First flight by the Wright brothers.
zh: 图1-3：莱特兄弟的首次飞行。

en: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
zh: 联邦航空管理局（FAA）的历史

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: During the early years of manned flight, aviation was a free for all because no government body was in place to establish policies or regulate and enforce safety standards. Individuals were free to conduct flights and operate aircraft with no government oversight. Most of the early flights were conducted for sport. Aviation was expensive and became the playground of the wealthy. Since these early airplanes were small, many people doubted their commercial value. One group of individuals believed otherwise and they became the genesis for modern airline travel.
zh: 在载人飞行的早期阶段，航空业对所有人来说都是"自由"的——因为当时还没有任何政府机构来制定政策、规范或执行安全标准。个人可以自行进行飞行活动，并操作飞机，而无需政府的监督。早期的飞行大多出于娱乐目的。由于航空成本较高，它最初只成为富人的专属领域。由于那些早期的飞机体积较小，许多人对其商业价值表示怀疑。然而，有一群人持不同观点，他们的努力最终促成了现代航空旅行的诞生。

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: P. E. Fansler, a Florida businessman living in St. Petersburg, approached Tom Benoist of the Benoist Aircraft Company in St. Louis, Missouri, about starting a flight route from St. Petersburg across the waterway to Tampa. Benoist suggested using his "Safety First" airboat and the two men signed an agreement for what would become the first scheduled airline in the United States. The first aircraft was delivered to St. Petersburg and made the first test flight on December 31, 1913.
zh: P.E. Fansler，一位居住在圣彼得堡（佛罗里达州）的商人，联系了位于密苏里州圣路易斯的Benoist飞机公司的Tom Benoist，讨论从圣彼得堡出发开辟一条新的航空航线的可能性。从圣彼得堡出发，通过水路前往坦帕。贝努瓦（Benoist）提议使用他的"安全第一"（Safety First）型气垫船；两人随后签署了一份协议，由此成立了美国历史上第一家定期运营的航空公司。首架飞机于1913年12月31日被交付到圣彼得堡，并完成了首次试飞。

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: A public auction decided who would win the honor of becoming the first paying airline customer. The former mayor of St. Petersburg, A. C. Pheil, made the winning bid of $400.00, which secured his place in history as the first paying airline passenger.
zh: 通过公开拍卖，最终确定了谁将获得成为首家付费航空乘客的荣誉。圣彼得堡的前市长A.C.Pheil提出了最高报价（400.00美元），从而确保了自己在历史上作为首位付费航空乘客的地位。

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: On January 1, 1914, the first scheduled airline flight was conducted. The flight length was 21 miles and lasted 23 minutes due to a headwind. The return trip took 20 minutes. The line, which was subsidized by Florida businessmen, continued for 4 months and offered regular passage for $5.00 per person or $5.00 per 100 pounds of cargo. Shortly after the opening of the line, Benoist added a new airboat that afforded more protection from spray during takeoff and landing. The routes were also extended to Manatee, Bradenton, and Sarasota giving further credence to the idea of a profitable commercial airline.
zh: 1914年1月1日，首次按计划进行的航空航班正式启程。该航班的飞行距离为21英里，由于遇到逆风，全程耗时23分钟；返程则仅用了20分钟。这条航线得到了佛罗里达州商人的资助，共运营了4个月，提供定期运输服务，收费标准为：每人5.00美元或每100磅货物收取5.00美元的费用。航线开通后不久，Benoist公司又新增了一艘新型气垫船，这种气垫船在起飞和降落时能更好地抵御水花飞溅的干扰。同时，航班路线也被延伸至马纳蒂（Manatee）、布拉登顿（Bradenton）和萨拉索塔（Sarasota）等地，进一步证明了这条航线具有盈利潜力的商业价值。

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: The St. Petersburg-Tampa Airboat Line continued throughout the winter months with flights finally being suspended when the winter tourist industry began to dry up. The airline operated for only 4 months, but 1,205 passengers were carried without injury. This experiment proved commercial passenger airline travel was viable.
zh: 圣彼得堡-坦帕的空气船航线在整个冬季期间都持续运营，直到冬季旅游产业开始萎缩时，航班服务才最终暂停。尽管该航空公司仅运营了4个月时间，但仍运送了1,205名乘客，且没有发生任何安全事故。这一尝试证明了商业客运航空旅行的可行性。

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: The advent of World War I offered the airplane a chance to demonstrate its varied capabilities. It began the war as a reconnaissance platform, but by 1918, airplanes were being mass produced to serve as fighters, bombers, trainers, as well as reconnaissance platforms.
zh: 第一次世界大战的爆发为飞机提供了展示其多种用途的机会。战争初期，飞机主要被用作侦察工具；但到了1918年，飞机开始大规模生产，用于执行战斗任务、轰炸任务、训练任务，同时也继续承担侦察职能。

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: Figure 1-4. Benoist airboat.
zh: 图1-4：Benoist气垫船。

metadata.section: History of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

en: Transcontinental Air Mail Route
zh: 跨大陆航空邮件航线

en: Airmail routes continued to expand until the Transcontinental Mail Route was inaugurated. [Figure 1-5] This route spanned from San Francisco to New York for a total distance of 2,612 miles with 13 intermediate stops along the way. [Figure 1-6] On May 20, 1926, Congress passed the Air Commerce Act, which served as the cornerstone for aviation within the United States. This legislation was supported by leaders in the aviation industry who felt that the airplane could not reach its full potential without assistance from the Federal Government in improving safety.
zh: 航空邮件的航线持续扩展，直到横贯大陆的邮政航线正式开通。[图1-5]这条航线从旧金山延伸至纽约，总里程为2,612英里，中途设有13个停靠点。[图1-6]1926年5月20日，国会通过了《航空商业法》，该法案成为美国航空业发展的基石。这一立法得到了航空业领袖们的支持；他们认为，如果没有联邦政府在提高安全性方面的帮助，飞机就无法充分发挥其潜力。

en: The Air Commerce Act charged the Secretary of Commerce with fostering air commerce, issuing and enforcing air traffic rules, licensing pilots, certificating aircraft, establishing airways, and operating and maintaining aids to air navigation. The Department of Commerce created a new Aeronautics Branch whose primary mission was to provide oversight for the aviation industry. In addition, the Aeronautics Branch took over the construction and operation of the nation's system of lighted airways. The Postal Service, as part of the Transcontinental Air Mail Route system, had initiated this system. The Department of Commerce made significant advances in aviation communications, including the introduction of radio beacons as an effective means of navigation.
zh: 《航空商业法》赋予商务部长以下职责：促进航空商业的发展，制定并执行空中交通规则，颁发飞行员执照，对飞机进行认证，建立航空航线，以及运营和维护航空导航辅助设施。商务部设立了一个新的"航空事务处"，其主要任务是对航空业实施监管。此外，"航空事务处"还接管了全国照明航空航线的建设与维护工作。邮政服务系统作为跨大陆航空邮件运输网络的一部分，最初负责建立了这一航空航线网络。商务部在航空通信领域取得了重大进展，其中包括引入无线电信标作为有效的导航手段。

en: Built at intervals of approximately 10 miles apart, the standard beacon tower was 51 feet high, and was topped with a powerful rotating light. Below the rotating light, two course lights pointed forward and back along the airway. The course lights flashed a code to identify the beacon's number. The tower usually stood in the center of a concrete arrow 70 feet long. A generator shed, where required, stood at the "feather" end of the arrow. [Figure 1-7]
zh: 这些标准信标塔每隔约10英里建造一座，高度为51英尺（约15.5米），顶部装有强大的旋转灯。在旋转灯的下方，还有两盏定向灯，它们沿着航线向前和向后照射。这两盏定向灯会闪烁特定的信号代码，以标识该信标塔的编号。通常情况下，信标塔会矗立在一个长度为70英尺（约21.3米）的混凝土"箭头"的中央；在必要时，发电机房会设置在这个"混凝土箭头"的末端。[图1-7]

en: Federal Certification of Pilots and Mechanics
zh: 联邦对飞行员和机械师的认证

metadata.section: Federal Certification of Pilots and Mechanics

en: The Aeronautics Branch of the Department of Commerce began pilot certification with the first license issued on April 6, 1927. The recipient was the Chief of the Aeronautics Branch, William P. MacCracken, Jr. [Figure 1-8] (Orville Wright, who was no longer an active flier, had declined the honor.) MacCracken's license was the first issued to a pilot by a civilian agency of the Federal Government. Some 3 months later, the Aeronautics Branch issued the first Federal aircraft mechanic license.
zh: 美国商务部下属的航空部门于1927年4月6日开始实施飞行认证制度，并颁发了首张执照。该执照的获得者是航空部门的负责人威廉·P·麦克拉克肯（William P. MacCracken, Jr.）[见图1-8]；奥维尔·莱特（Orville Wright）虽然已不再从事飞行活动，但他拒绝了这一荣誉。麦克拉克肯获得的执照是美国联邦政府下属民用机构颁发的首张飞行员执照。大约三个月后，该航空部门又颁发了首张联邦航空机械师执照。

metadata.section: Federal Certification of Pilots and Mechanics

en: Equally important for safety was the establishment of a system of certification for aircraft. On March 29, 1927, the Aeronautics Branch issued the first airworthiness type certificate to the Buhl Airster CA-3, a three-place open biplane.
zh: 对于安全而言，同样重要的是建立一套飞机认证体系。1927年3月29日，航空部门向Buhl Airster CA-3（一款可容纳三人的开放式双翼飞机）颁发了首张适航证书。

metadata.section: Federal Certification of Pilots and Mechanics

en: In 1934, to recognize the tremendous strides made in aviation and to display the enhanced status within the department, the Aeronautics Branch was renamed the Bureau of Air Commerce. [Figure 1-9] Within this time frame, the Bureau of Air Commerce brought together a group of airlines and encouraged them to form the first three Air Traffic Control (ATC) facilities along the established air routes. Then in 1936, the Bureau of Air Commerce took over the responsibilities of operating the centers and continued to advance the ATC facilities. ATC has come a long way from the early controllers using maps, chalkboards, and performing mental math calculations in order to separate aircraft along flight routes.
zh: 1934年，为了表彰航空领域所取得的巨大进步，并彰显该部门在政府体系中的重要地位，航空局（Aeronautics Branch）被更名为航空商务局（Bureau of Air Commerce）[见图1-9]。在这一时期，航空商务局整合了一批航空公司，并鼓励他们在已建立的航空路线上建立首批三个空中交通管制（ATC）设施。随后，在1936年，航空商务局接管了这些设施的运营职责，并继续推进ATC系统的发展。与早期那些仅依靠地图、黑板以及进行心算来协调飞机飞行路线的管制人员相比，如今的ATC技术已经取得了巨大的进步。

metadata.section: Federal Certification of Pilots and Mechanics

en: The photograph was taken in 1933. During Vidal's tenure, the Aeronautics Branch was renamed the Bureau of Air Commerce on July 1, 1934. The new name more accurately reflected the status of the organization within the Department of Commerce.
zh: 这张照片拍摄于1933年。在维达尔的任职期间，航空部门于1934年7月1日更名为"航空商务局"（Bureau of Air Commerce）。这一新名称更准确地反映了该组织在商务部（Department of Commerce）中的地位。

metadata.section: Federal Certification of Pilots and Mechanics

en: The Civil Aeronautics Act of 1938
zh: 1938年《民用航空法》

en: In 1938, the Civil Aeronautics Act transferred the civil aviation responsibilities to a newly created, independent body, named the Civil Aeronautics Authority (CAA). This Act empowered the CAA to regulate airfares and establish new routes for the airlines to service.
zh: 1938年，《民用航空法》将民用航空领域的职责移交给了一个新成立的独立机构——民用航空管理局（Civil Aeronautics Authority，简称CAA）。该法律赋予CAA监管机票价格以及为航空公司开辟新的航线服务的权力。

en: President Franklin Roosevelt split the CAA into two agencies—the Civil Aeronautics Administration (CAA) and the Civil Aeronautics Board (CAB). Both agencies were still part of the Department of Commerce but the CAB functioned independently of the Secretary of Commerce. The role of the CAA was to facilitate ATC, certification of airmen and aircraft, rule enforcement, and the development of new airways. The CAB was charged with rule making to enhance safety, accident investigation, and the economic regulation of the airlines. Then in 1946, Congress gave the CAA the responsibility of administering the Federal Aid Airport Program. This program was designed to promote the establishment of civil airports throughout the country.
zh: 富兰克林·罗斯福总统将《民用航空法》（CAA）拆分为两个机构：民用航空管理局（Civil Aeronautics Administration，简称CAA）和民用航空委员会（Civil Aeronautics Board，简称CAB）。这两个机构仍隶属于商务部，但民用航空委员会的运作独立于商务部部长。民用航空管理局的职责包括协调空中交通管制（ATC）、飞行员及飞机的认证、法规的执行以及新航线的开发；而民用航空委员会则负责制定相关法规以提升飞行安全、开展事故调查，并对航空公司进行经济监管。1946年，国会授权民用航空管理局负责管理联邦援助计划机场建设项目。该计划旨在促进全国范围内民用机场的建设与发展。

en: The Federal Aviation Act of 1958
zh: 1958年《联邦航空法》

en: By mid-century, air traffic had increased and jet aircraft had been introduced into the civil aviation arena. A series of mid-air collisions underlined the need for more regulation of the aviation industry. Aircraft were not only increasing in numbers, but were now streaking across the skies at much higher speeds. The Federal Aviation Act of 1958 established a new independent body that assumed the roles of the CAA and transferred the rule making authority of the CAB to the newly created Federal Aviation Agency (FAA). In addition, the FAA was given complete control of the common civil-military system of air navigation and ATC. The man who was given the honor of being the first Administrator of the FAA was former Air Force General Elwood Richard "Pete" Quesada. He served as the administrator from 1959-1961. [Figure 1-10]
zh: 到20世纪中叶，航空交通量显著增加，喷气式飞机也被引入了民用航空领域。一系列发生在空中的碰撞事件凸显了对航空行业加强监管的必要性。不仅飞机的数量在不断增加，它们的飞行速度也大幅提高。1958年颁布的《联邦航空法》设立了一个新的独立机构，该机构接管了原CAA（Civil Aviation Authority）的职责，并将CAB（Civil Aviation Board）的规则制定权移交给了新成立的联邦航空管理局（FAA）。此外，FAA还被赋予了对民用与军用航空导航系统及空中交通管制（ATC）的全面控制权。首位担任FAA局长职务的人是前空军将领埃尔伍德·理查德·"皮特"·克萨达（Elwood Richard "Pete" Quesada），他的任期从1959年持续到1961年。[图1-10]

en: Department of Transportation (DOT)
zh: 交通运输部 (Department of Transportation, 简称 DOT)

en: On October 15, 1966, Congress established the Department of Transportation (DOT), which was given oversight of the transportation industry within the United States. The result was a combination of both air and surface transportation. Its mission was and is to serve the United States by ensuring a fast, safe, efficient, accessible, and convenient transportation system meeting vital national interests and enhancing the quality of life of the American people, then, now, and into the future. The DOT began operation on April 1, 1967. At this same time, the Federal Aviation Agency was renamed to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
zh: 1966年10月15日，国会成立了交通部（Department of Transportation，简称DOT），该部门负责监管美国的交通运输行业。这一机构的职责涵盖了航空运输与地面交通两大领域。其使命始终如一：通过提供快速、安全、高效、便捷的交通运输服务来服务于美国人民，从而维护国家的根本利益，并提升美国民众的生活质量——无论是过去、现在，还是未来。1967年4月1日，交通部正式开始运作。同年，联邦航空局（Federal Aviation Agency）更名为联邦航空管理局（Federal Aviation Administration，简称FAA）。

en: The role of the CAB was assumed by the newly created National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB), which was charged with the investigation of all transportation accidents within the United States.
zh: CAB（National Transportation Safety Board）的职责由新成立的美国国家运输安全委员会（NTSB）承担，该委员会负责调查美国境内发生的所有交通运输事故。

en: As aviation continued to grow, the FAA took on additional duties and responsibilities. With the highjacking epidemic of the 1960s, the FAA was responsible for increasing the security duties of aviation both on the ground and in the air. After September 11, 2001, the duties were transferred to a newly created body called the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).
zh: 随着航空业的持续发展，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）承担了更多的职责和任务。在20世纪60年代劫机事件频发的背景下，FAA负责加强航空领域在地面和空中的安全措施。2001年9月11日之后，这些职责被移交给了一个新成立的机构——国土安全部（DHS）。

en: With numerous aircraft flying in and out of larger cities, the FAA began to concentrate on the environmental aspect of aviation by establishing and regulating the noise standards of aircraft. Additionally, in the 1960s and 1970s, the FAA began to regulate high altitude (over 500 feet) kite and balloon flying. In 1970, more duties were assumed by the FAA in the addition of a new federal airport aid program and increased responsibility for airport safety.
zh: 随着大量飞机在各大城市之间往返飞行，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）开始关注航空业对环境的影响，并制定了相应的噪音标准来规范飞机的运行。此外，在20世纪60年代和70年代，FAA还开始对高空（500英尺以上）飞行的风筝和热气球进行监管。1970年，FAA的职责范围进一步扩大，新增了一项联邦机场援助计划，并承担了更多保障机场安全的责任。

en: ATC Automation
zh: ATC自动化

en: By the mid-1970s, the FAA had achieved a semi-automated ATC system based on a marriage of radar and computer technology. By automating certain routine tasks, the system allowed controllers to concentrate more efficiently on the vital task of providing aircraft separation. Data appearing directly on the controllers' scopes provided the identity, altitude, and groundspeed of aircraft carrying radar beacons. Despite its effectiveness, this system required enhancement to keep pace with the increased air traffic of the late 1970s. The increase was due in part to the competitive environment created by the Airline Deregulation Act of 1978. This law phased out CAB's economic regulation of the airlines, and CAB ceased to exist at the end of 1984.
zh: 到20世纪70年代中期，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）已经开发出一种半自动的空中交通管制（ATC）系统，该系统结合了雷达技术和计算机技术。通过自动化某些常规任务，该系统使空中交通管制员能够更高效地完成确保飞机之间安全间隔的关键工作。显示在管制员视野中的数据包含了装有雷达信标的飞机的身份、飞行高度以及地面速度等信息。尽管这一系统非常有效，但为了应对20世纪70年代末日益增长的航空流量，仍需对其进行改进。航空流量的增加部分是由于1978年《航空公司解除管制法案》所营造的竞争环境所致。该法案逐步取消了联邦民用航空局（CAB）对航空公司的经济监管措施，而联邦民用航空局本身也在1984年底正式撤销。

en: To meet the challenge of traffic growth, the FAA unveiled the National Airspace System (NAS) Plan in January 1982. The new plan called for more advanced systems for en route and terminal ATC, modernized flight service stations, and improvements in ground-to-air surveillance and communication.
zh: 为了应对交通增长的挑战，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）于1982年1月发布了《国家空域系统（NAS）计划》。该新计划要求采用更先进的空中交通管制（ATC）系统，对飞行服务站进行现代化改造，并提升地面与空中之间的监控和通信能力。

en: The Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) Strike
zh: 专业空中交通管制员组织（PATCO）的罢工

metadata.section: The Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) Strike

en: While preparing the NAS Plan, the FAA faced a strike by key members of its workforce. An earlier period of discord between management and the Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) culminated in a 1970 "sickout" by 3,000 controllers. Although controllers subsequently gained additional wage and retirement benefits, another period of tension led to an illegal strike in August 1981. The government dismissed over 11,000 strike participants and decertified PATCO. By the spring of 1984, the FAA ended the last of the special restrictions imposed to keep the airspace system operating safely during the strike.
zh: 在制定国家航空系统（NAS）计划的过程中，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）遭遇了其核心员工群体的罢工。此前，管理层与专业航空人员之间就已经存在分歧和矛盾。美国空中交通管制员组织（PATCO）曾在1970年引发了一场大规模的罢工行动，当时共有3,000名管制员参与了这次罢工。尽管此后这些管制员的工资和退休福利得到了改善，但紧张局势再度出现，最终导致他们在1981年8月发动了非法罢工。政府解雇了超过11,000名参与罢工的员工，并取消了PATCO的官方认证资格。到了1984年春季，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）终于废除了为保障罢工期间空域系统安全而实施的所有特殊限制措施。

metadata.section: The Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) Strike

en: Until 1978, the CAB regulated many areas of commercial aviation such as fares, routes, and schedules. The Airline Deregulation Act of 1978, however, removed many of these controls, thus changing the face of civil aviation in the United States. After deregulation, unfettered free competition ushered in a new era in passenger air travel.
zh: 直到1978年，美国民用航空委员会（CAB）还负责监管商业航空的许多方面，包括票价、航线和航班时刻表。然而，1978年颁布的《航空公司放松管制法案》取消了这些管制措施，从而彻底改变了美国民用航空业的面貌。放松管制后，不受限制的自由竞争开启了乘客航空旅行的新时代。

metadata.section: The Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) Strike

en: The CAB had three main functions: to award routes to airlines, to limit the entry of air carriers into new markets, and to regulate fares for passengers. Much of the established practices of commercial passenger travel within the United States went back to the policies of Walter Folger Brown, the United States Postmaster General during the administration of President Herbert Hoover. Brown had changed the mail payments system to encourage the manufacture of passenger aircraft instead of mail-carrying aircraft. His influence was crucial in awarding contracts and helped create four major domestic airlines: United, American, Eastern, and Transcontinental and Western Air (TWA). Similarly, Brown had also helped give Pan American a monopoly on international routes.
zh: CAB（Civil Aviation Board）主要有三项职能：向航空公司分配航线资源、限制新航空运营商进入市场，以及规范乘客的票价。美国国内商业客运领域的许多现行做法其实都可以追溯到沃尔特·福尔杰·布朗（Walter Folger Brown）的政策——他在赫伯特·胡佛总统执政期间担任美国邮政总局局长。布朗改革了邮件支付制度，旨在鼓励航空公司生产用于运送乘客的飞机而非仅用于运输邮件的飞机。他的影响力在合同授予过程中起到了关键作用，并促成了四家大型国内航空公司的诞生：联合航空公司（United Airlines）、美国航空公司（American Airlines）、东方航空公司（Eastern Airlines）以及大陆航空公司和西部航空公司（Transcontinental and Western Air, TWA）。同样，布朗还帮助泛美航空公司（Pan American Airlines）获得了国际航线的垄断权。

metadata.section: The Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) Strike

en: The push to deregulate, or at least to reform the existing laws governing passenger carriers, was accelerated by President Jimmy Carter, who appointed economist and former professor Alfred Kahn, a vocal supporter of deregulation, to head the CAB. A second force to deregulate emerged from abroad. In 1977, Freddie Laker, a British entrepreneur who owned Laker Airways, created the Skytrain service, which offered extraordinarily cheap fares for transatlantic flights. Laker's offerings coincided with a boom in low-cost domestic flights as the CAB eased some limitations on charter flights (i.e., flights offered by companies that do not actually own planes but leased them from the major airlines). The big air carriers responded by proposing their own lower fares. For example, American Airlines, the country's second largest airline, obtained CAB approval for "SuperSaver" tickets.
zh: 推动放松管制（或至少改革现行规范客运航空公司的法律）的进程，在吉米·卡特总统任内得到了加速。卡特任命了经济学家兼前教授阿尔弗雷德·卡恩来领导美国民航管理局（CAB），而阿尔弗雷德·卡恩正是放松管制的坚定支持者。另一个推动放松管制的力量来自国外：1977年，拥有Laker Airways公司的英国企业家弗雷迪·莱克推出了Skytrain服务，该服务提供了极其优惠的跨大西洋航班票价。与此同时，随着美国民航管理局放宽了对包机航班（即那些并不实际拥有飞机、而是从大型航空公司租用飞机的公司所提供的航班）的一些限制，国内低成本航班市场也迎来了繁荣发展。各大航空公司对此作出了回应，纷纷推出了自己的低价机票产品。例如，作为美国第二大航空公司的美国航空公司（American Airlines），获得了民航管理局的批准，得以推出"SuperSaver"票价计划。

metadata.section: The Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) Strike

en: All of these events proved to be favorable for large-scale deregulation. In November 1977, Congress formally deregulated air cargo. In late 1978, Congress passed the Airline Deregulation Act of 1978, legislation that had been principally authored by Senators Edward Kennedy and Howard Cannon. [Figure 1-11] There was stiff opposition to the bill—from the major airlines who feared free competition, from labor unions who feared non-union employees, and from safety advocates who feared that safety would be sacrificed. Public support was, however, strong enough to pass the act. The act appeased the major airlines by offering generous subsidies and pleased workers by offering high unemployment benefits if they lost their jobs as a result. The most important effect of the act, whose laws were slowly phased in, was on the passenger market. For the first time in 40 years, airlines could enter the market or (from 1981) expand their routes as they saw fit. Airlines (from 1982) also had full freedom to set their fares. In 1984, the CAB was finally abolished since its primary duty of regulating the airline industry was no longer necessary.
zh: 所有这些事件都被证明有利于大规模的放松管制（即减少政府对市场的干预）。1977年11月，国会正式取消了航空货运领域的管制。1978年底，国会通过了《1978年航空公司放松管制法案》（Airline Deregulation Act of 1978），该法案主要由参议员爱德华·肯尼迪（Edward Kennedy）和霍华德·坎农（Howard Cannon）起草。[图1-11]这项法案遭到了强烈的反对：主要航空公司担心会引发自由竞争；工会则担心非工会员工的出现会对他们的工作环境产生障碍，有安全倡导者表示担忧，担心安全性会因此受到牺牲。然而，公众的支持度足以使该法案获得通过。该法案通过提供慷慨的补贴来安抚主要航空公司，并在员工因失业而失去工作时为他们提供高额的失业救济金来让他们感到满意。这项法案最重要的影响体现在乘客市场方面：40年来首次，航空公司可以自由进入市场或（从1981年起）根据自身需要扩展航线；同时，航空公司也从1982年开始完全拥有制定票价的自主权。最终，在1984年，该监管机构（CAB）被废除——因为其原本负责监管航空业的主要职责已不再必要。

metadata.section: The Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization (PATCO) Strike

en: The Role of the FAA
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的角色

en: The Code of Federal Regulations (CFR)
zh: 《联邦法规》（Code of Federal Regulations，简称CFR）

en: The FAA is empowered by regulations to promote aviation safety and establish safety standards for civil aviation. The FAA achieves these objectives under the Code of Federal Regulations (CFR), which is the codification of the general and permanent rules published by the executive departments and agencies of the United States Government. The regulations are divided into 50 different codes, called Titles, that represent broad areas subject to Federal regulation. FAA regulations are listed under Title 14, "Aeronautics and Space," which encompasses all aspects of civil aviation from how to earn a pilot's certificate to maintenance of an aircraft. Title 14 CFR Chapter 1, Federal Aviation Administration, is broken down into subchapters A through N as illustrated in Figure 1-12.
zh: 根据相关法规，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）被授权促进航空安全并为民用航空制定安全标准。FAA通过《联邦法规》（Code of Federal Regulations, CFR）来实现这些目标。《联邦法规》是美国政府各行政部门和机构发布的通用性、永久性规则的汇编。这些法规被分为50个不同的部分，每个部分被称为"标题"（Title），它们涵盖了需要接受联邦监管的广泛领域。FAA的法规收录在《联邦法规》第14篇"航空与航天"（Aeronautics and Space）中，该篇章涵盖了民用航空的所有方面，从如何获得飞行员执照到飞机的维护等。第14篇《联邦法规》的第1章"联邦航空管理局"（Federal Aviation Administration）进一步细分为A至N等多个子章节，具体内容如图1-12所示。

en: For the pilot, certain parts of 14 CFR are more relevant than others. During flight training, it is helpful for the pilot to become familiar with the parts and subparts that relate to flight training and pilot certification. For instance, 14 CFR part 61 pertains to the certification of pilots, flight instructors, and ground instructors. It also defines the eligibility, aeronautical knowledge, and flight proficiency, as well as training and testing requirements for each type of pilot certificate issued. 14 CFR part 91 provides guidance in the areas of general flight rules, visual flight rules (VFR), and instrument flight rules (IFR), while 14 CFR part 43 covers aircraft maintenance, preventive maintenance, rebuilding, and alterations.
zh: 对于飞行员来说，14CFR（联邦航空法规）中的某些部分比其他部分更为重要。在飞行训练期间，对于飞行员来说，熟悉与飞行训练和飞行员认证相关的各个部分及子部分是非常有帮助的。例如，《联邦法规》（14CFR）第61部分涉及飞行员、飞行教员和地面教员的认证工作；该部分还规定了各类飞行员证书的颁发条件、航空知识要求、飞行技能标准，以及相应的培训与考核要求。《联邦法规》第91部分为通用飞行规则、目视飞行规则（VFR）和仪表飞行规则（IFR）的相关内容提供了指导；而第43部分则涵盖了飞机的维护工作，包括预防性维护、大修及改装等方面。

en: Primary Locations of the FAA
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的主要办公地点

en: The FAA headquarters are in Washington, DC, and there are nine regional offices strategically located across the United States. The agency's two largest field facilities are the Mike Monroney Aeronautical Center (MMAC) in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, and the William J. Hughes Technical Center (WJHTC) in Atlantic City, New Jersey. Home to FAA training and logistics services, the MMAC provides a number of aviation safety-related and business support services. The WJHTC is the premier aviation research and development and test and evaluation facility in the country. The center's programs include testing and evaluation in ATC, communication, navigation, airports, aircraft safety, and security. Furthermore, the WJHTC is active in long-range development of innovative aviation systems and concepts, development of new ATC equipment and software, and modification of existing systems and procedures.
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的总部设在华盛顿特区，同时在美国各地战略性地设立了九个地区办事处。该机构规模最大的两个实地设施分别是位于俄克拉荷马州俄克拉荷马城的迈克·蒙罗尼航空中心（Mike Monroney Aeronautical Center, MMAC），以及位于新泽西州大西洋城的威廉·J·休斯技术中心（William J. Hughes Technical Center, WJHTC）。作为FAA培训与物流服务的核心基地，MMAC提供多种与航空安全相关的服务以及业务支持；而WJHTC则是该国最先进的航空研究与开发、测试及评估机构。该中心的业务范围涵盖空中交通管制（ATC）、通信系统、导航技术、机场运营、飞机安全性以及安保措施等多个领域，并在相开发创新的航空系统和概念，研制新的空中交通管制（ATC）设备和软件，并对现有的系统和程序进行改进发挥着重要作用。

en: Field Offices
zh: 外地办事处（Field Offices）

en: Flight Standards Service
zh: 飞行标准服务局（Flight Standards Service）

en: Within the FAA, the Flight Standards Service promotes safe air transportation by setting the standards for certification and oversight of airmen, air operators, air agencies, and designees. It also promotes safety of flight of civil aircraft and air commerce by:
zh: 在美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）内部，飞行标准服务部门通过制定飞行员、航空运营商、航空机构及相关人员的认证与监管标准来促进安全的空中运输。此外，该部门还通过以下措施来保障民用飞机的飞行安全及航空商业活动的顺利进行：

en: Accomplishing certification, inspection, surveillance, investigation, and enforcement.
zh: 完成认证、检查、监督、调查以及执法等工作。

en: Managing the system for registration of civil aircraft and all airmen records.
zh: 负责管理民用航空器的注册系统以及所有飞行人员的记录。

en: Flight Standards District Office (FSDO)
zh: 飞行标准区办公室（Flight Standards District Office，简称FSDO）

en: The FAA has approximately 80 FSDOs. [Figure 1-13] These offices provide information and services for the aviation community. FSDO phone numbers are listed in the telephone directory under Government Offices, DOT, FAA. Another convenient method of finding a local office is to use the FSDO locator available at: www.faa.gov/about/office_organ/field_offices/fsdo.
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）大约设有80个地方服务办公室（FSDOs，见图1-13）。这些办公室为航空业界提供信息和服务。各地方服务办公室的电话号码可以在电话簿中"Government Offices, DOT, FAA"这一分类下找到。另一种方便查找当地服务办公室的方法是使用FAA提供的在线查询工具：www.faa.gov/about/office_organ/field_offices/fsdo。

en: In addition to accident investigation and the enforcement of aviation regulations, the FSDO is also responsible for the certification and surveillance of air carriers, air operators, flight schools/training centers, and airmen including pilots and flight instructors. Each FSDO is staffed by Aviation Safety Inspectors (ASIs) who play a key role in making the nation's aviation system safe.
zh: 除了事故调查和航空法规的执行外，联邦航空管理局（FSDO）还负责对航空公司、航空运营商、飞行学校/培训机构以及包括飞行员和飞行教员在内的航空人员的认证和监管工作。每个联邦航空管理局都配备了航空安全检查员（ASIs），他们在确保国家航空系统的安全性方面发挥着关键作用。

en: Aviation Safety Inspector (ASI)
zh: 航空安全检查员（ASI）

en: The ASIs administer and enforce safety regulations and standards for the production, operation, maintenance, and/or modification of aircraft used in civil aviation. They also specialize in conducting inspections of various aspects of the aviation system, such as aircraft and parts manufacturing, aircraft operation, aircraft airworthiness, and cabin safety. ASIs must complete a training program at the FAA Academy in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, which includes airman evaluation and pilot testing techniques and procedures. ASIs also receive extensive on-the-job training and recurrent training on a regular basis. The FAA has approximately 3,700 inspectors located in its FSDO offices. All questions concerning pilot certification (and/or requests for other aviation information or services) should be directed to the local FSDO.
zh: 航空安全检查员（ASIs）负责管理和执行与民用航空中使用的飞机的生产、运营、维护及/或改装相关的安全法规和标准。他们还专门从事对航空系统各个方面的检查工作，包括飞机及其零部件的制造、飞机的运行状态、飞机的适航性以及客舱安全等。这些检查员必须在位于俄克拉荷马州奥克拉荷马城的FAA学院完成培训课程，该课程内容包括对飞行人员的评估以及飞行员测试的技术和程序。此外，他们还会定期接受大量的在职培训和复训。目前，FAA在其FSDO（Flight Standards Division）办公室共配备了约3,700名检查员。所有与飞行员执照认证相关的问题（或对其他航空信息/服务的需求），都应咨询当地的FSDO部门。

en: FAA Safety Team (FAASTeam)
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局安全团队（FAA Safety Team，简称FAASTeam）

en: The FAA is dedicated to improving the safety of United States civilian aviation by conveying safety principles and practices through training, outreach, and education. The FAA Safety Team (FAASTeam) exemplifies this commitment. The FAASTeam has replaced the Aviation Safety Program (ASP), whose education of airmen on all types of safety subjects successfully reduced accidents. Its success led to its demise because the easy-to-fix accident causes have been addressed. To take aviation safety one step further, Flight Standards Service created the FAASTeam, which is devoted to reducing aircraft accidents by using a coordinated effort to focus resources on elusive accident causes.
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）致力于通过培训、宣传和教育活动来推广安全原则与实践，从而提升美国民用航空的安全水平。FAA的安全团队（FAASTeam）正是这一承诺的典范。该团队取代了之前的"航空安全计划"（Aviation Safety Program, ASP），后者通过对飞行员进行各类安全主题的教育成功减少了事故的发生率。然而，随着那些容易解决的事故原因已被有效消除，"航空安全计划"也随之被废止。为了进一步提升航空安全水平，FAA成立了FAASTeam，该团队通过协调各方资源，专门针对那些难以发现或难以解决的事故根源开展工作，以进一步减少飞机事故的发生。

en: Each of the FAA's nine regions has a Regional FAASTeam Office dedicated to this new safety program and managed by the Regional FAASTeam Manager (RFM). The FAASTeam is "teaming" up with individuals and the aviation industry to create a unified effort against accidents and tip the safety culture in the right direction. To learn more about this effort to improve aviation safety, to take a course at their online learning center, or to join the FAASTeam, visit their website at www.faasafety.gov.
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的九个地区每个都设有一个专门的区域FAASTeam办公室，负责实施这一新的安全计划，这些办公室由区域FAASTeam经理（RFM）进行管理。FAASTeam正与相关个人及航空业合作伙伴紧密合作，共同推动各项安全工作的开展，以实现统一的安全标准与协作机制。这些努力旨在预防事故，并推动安全文化的健康发展。如想了解更多关于提升航空安全的举措，您可以在他们的在线学习中心参加相关课程，或加入FAASTeam。详情请访问他们的官方网站：www.faasafety.gov。

en: Obtaining Assistance from the FAA
zh: 从美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）获得帮助

en: Information can be obtained from the FAA by phone, Internet/e-mail, or mail. To talk to the FAA toll-free 24 hours a day, call 1-866-TELL-FAA (1-866-835-5322). To visit the FAA's website, go to www.faa.gov. Individuals can also e-mail an FAA representative at a local FSDO office by accessing the staff e-mail address available via the "Contact FAA" link at the bottom of the FAA home page. Letters can be sent to:
zh: 相关信息可以通过电话、互联网或电子邮件从美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）获取。如需全天24小时免费联系FAA，请拨打1-866-TELL-FAA（1-866-835-5322）。如需访问FAA的官方网站，请访问www.faa.gov。个人也可以通过"Contact FAA"页面获取当地FSDO办公室工作人员的电子邮件地址，并直接发送邮件给相关代表。

en:
Federal Aviation Administration
800 Independence Ave, SW
Washington, DC 20591
zh:
美国联邦航空管理局
800 Independence Ave, SW
华盛顿特区，邮编：20591

en: The FAA provides a variety of important reference material for the student, as well as the advanced civil aviation pilot. In addition to the regulations provided online by the FAA, several other publications are available to the user. Almost all reference material is available online at www.faa.gov in downloadable format. Commercial aviation publishers also provide published and online reference material to further aid the aviation pilot.
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）为学生以及高级民用航空飞行员提供了多种重要的参考资料。除了FAA在线发布的法规外，用户还可以获取其他几份出版物。几乎所有参考资料都可以在www.faa.gov网站上以可下载的格式找到。商业航空出版商也提供了印刷版和在线版的参考资料，以进一步帮助航空飞行员。

en: Aeronautical Information Manual (AIM)
zh: 航空信息手册（Aeronautical Information Manual，简称AIM）

en: The Aeronautical Information Manual (AIM) is the official guide to basic flight information and ATC procedures for the aviation community flying in the NAS of the United States. [Figure 1-14] An international version, containing parallel information as well as specific information on international airports, is also available. The AIM also contains information of interest to pilots, such as health and medical facts, flight safety, a pilot/controller glossary of terms used in the system, and information on safety, accidents, and reporting of hazards. This manual is offered for sale on a subscription basis or is available online at: http://bookstore.gpo.gov.
zh: 《航空信息手册》（Aeronautical Information Manual，简称AIM）是美国国家航空航天局（NASA）管辖范围内的航空社区使用的基本飞行信息及空中交通管制（ATC）程序的官方指南。[图1-14]该手册还提供国际版本，其中包含平行展示的信息以及关于国际机场的详细资料。AIM中还包含了飞行员感兴趣的内容，例如健康与医疗相关知识、飞行安全信息、飞行员/空中交通管制员使用的专业术语表，以及有关安全事项和事故处理的相关信息。本手册提供订阅服务，也可在线获取，网址为：http://bookstore.gpo.gov。

en: Order forms are provided at the beginning of the manual or online and should be sent to the Superintendent of Documents, United States Government Printing Office (GPO). The AIM is complemented by other operational publications that are available via separate subscriptions or online.
zh: 订单表格可以在手册的开头部分找到，也可以在线获取。填写完成后应将其发送给美国政府印刷局（United States Government Printing Office, GPO）的文件主管部门。AIM还配备了其他操作指南类出版物，这些出版物可以通过单独订阅或在线方式获得。

en: Handbooks
zh: 手册 (Handbooks)

en: Handbooks are developed to provide specific information about a particular topic that enhances training or understanding. The FAA publishes a variety of handbooks that generally fall into three categories: aircraft, aviation, and examiners and inspectors. [Figure 1-15] These handbooks can be purchased from the Superintendent of Documents or downloaded at www.faa.gov/regulations_policies. Aviation handbooks are also published by various commercial aviation companies. Aircraft flight manuals commonly called Pilot Operating Handbooks (POH) are documents developed by the airplane manufacturer, approved by the FAA, and are specific to a particular make and model aircraft by serial number. This subject is covered in greater detail in Chapter 8, "Flight Manuals and Other Documents," of this handbook. [Figure 1-16]
zh: 手册的编写目的是提供关于某一特定主题的详细信息，以帮助提升培训效果或加深理解。美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）发布了多种手册，这些手册大致可以分为三类：与飞机相关的手册、与航空运营相关的手册，以及针对考官和检查员的手册。[图1-15]这些手册可以从文件管理部门购买，也可以在www.faa.gov/regulations_policies网站上下载。此外，还有专门针对航空运营方面的手册，这些飞行手册由多家商业航空公司出版。通常被称为《飞行员操作手册》（Pilot Operating Handbook，简称POH）的文件，是由飞机制造商编写、经美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）批准发布的，并且专门针对特定型号的飞机（通过序列号进行区分）。关于这一主题的更详细内容，请参阅本手册的第8章"飞行手册及其他文件"。[图1-16]

en: Advisory Circulars (ACs)
zh: 咨询通告（Advisory Circulates，简称ACs）

en: An AC is an informational document that the FAA wants to distribute to the aviation community.
zh: AC（Airworthiness Certificate）是一份信息性文件，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）希望将其分发给航空界。

en: There are three parts to an AC number, as in 25-42C. The first part of the number identifies the subject matter area of the AC and corresponds to the appropriate 14 CFR part. For example, an AC on "Certification: Pilots and Flight and Ground Instructors" is numbered as AC 61-65E. Since ACs are numbered sequentially within each subject area, the second part of the number beginning with the dash identifies this sequence. The third part of the number is a letter assigned by the originating office and shows the revision sequence if an AC is revised. The first version of an AC does not have a revision letter. In Figure 1-17, this is the fifth revision, as designated by the "E."
zh: AC（行政法规）编号由三部分组成，例如"25-42C"。编号的第一部分用于标识该行政法规所涉及的主题领域，并对应相应的《联邦法规》（CFR）章节（共14个章节）。例如，关于"认证：飞行员及飞行教员和地面教员"的行政法规编号为"AC 61-65E"。由于同一主题领域的行政法规是按顺序编号的，因此编号的第二部分以破折号开头，用于表示该法规在序列中的位置。编号的第三部分是一个由发布机构指定的字母，用于表明该行政法规是否经过了修订；如果经过修订，则会使用相应的修订标识字母。一个行政法规的第一个版本没有修订标识字母。如图1-17所示，当前版本为第五次修订，对应的标识字母为"E"。

en: Flight Publications
zh: 飞行出版物（Flight Publications）

en: The FAA, in concert with other government agencies, orchestrates the publication and changes to publications that are key to safe flight. Figure 1-18 illustrates some publications a pilot may use.
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）与其他政府机构合作，负责发布和修订对飞行安全至关重要的各类文件。图1-18展示了飞行员可能会使用的一些相关文件。

en: Pilot and Aeronautical Information
zh: 飞行员与航空信息

en: Notices to Airmen (NOTAMs)
zh: 航空人员通知（NOTAMs）

en: Notices to Airmen, or NOTAMs, are time-critical aeronautical information either temporary in nature or not sufficiently known in advance to permit publication on aeronautical charts or in other operational publications. The information receives immediate dissemination via the National Notice to Airmen (NOTAM) System. NOTAMs contain current notices to airmen that are considered essential to the safety of flight, as well as supplemental data affecting other operational publications. There are many different reasons that NOTAMs are issued. Following are some of those reasons: Hazards, such as air shows, parachute jumps, kite flying, and rocket launches.
zh: "通知飞行员"（Notices to Airmen，简称NOTAM）属于具有时效性的航空信息。这类信息可能是临时性的，也可能因为发布时间过于紧迫而无法提前被纳入航空图表或其他操作性出版物中。此类信息会通过国家飞行员通知系统（National Notice to Airmen System）立即进行传播。NOTAM包含了对飞行安全至关重要的当前通知内容，同时也涵盖了可能影响其他操作性出版物的补充数据。发布NOTAM的原因有很多，以下是一些常见的原因：高空飞行、跳伞、放风筝以及火箭发射等。

en: Notifications of runway/taxiway/apron status with respect to snow, ice, and standing water (a SNOWTAM)
zh: 关于跑道/滑行道/停机坪状态的通知（包括积雪、结冰和积水情况），即SNOWTAM（Snow, Ice, and Standing Water Notification）。

en: Notification of an operationally significant change in volcanic ash or other dust contamination (an ASHTAM)
zh: 关于火山灰或其他粉尘污染（即ASHTAM）发生具有操作意义的变化的通知

en: Software code risk announcements with associated patches to reduce specific vulnerabilities
zh: 关于软件代码风险的公告，以及为降低特定漏洞而发布的相应补丁。

en: NOTAM information is generally classified into four categories: NOTAM (D) or NOTAMs that receive distant dissemination, distant and Flight Data Center (FDC) NOTAMs, Pointer NOTAMs, and Military NOTAMs pertaining to military airports or NAVAIDs that are part of the NAS. NOTAMs are available through Flight Service Station (FSS), Direct User Access Terminal Service (DUATS), private vendors, and many online websites.
zh: NOTAM（航行通告）信息通常被分为四类：通过远程传播方式发布的NOTAM（D类），由飞行数据中心（Flight Data Center, FDC）发布的NOTAM，用于提供导航指引的NOTAM（称为"指针型NOTAM"），以及与军事机场或属于导航辅助系统（Navigation Aid System, NAS）组成部分的导航设施相关的军事NOTAM。这些NOTAM可以通过飞行服务站（Flight Service Station, FSS）、直接用户访问终端服务（Direct User Access Terminal Service, DUATS）、私营供应商，以及许多在线网站获取。

en: Aircraft Classifications and Ultralight Vehicles
zh: 飞机分类与超轻型飞行器

en: The FAA uses various ways to classify or group machines operated or flown in the air. The most general grouping uses the term aircraft. This term is in 14 CFR 1.1 and means a device that is used or intended to be used for flight in the air.
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）采用多种方法对在空中运行或飞行的飞行器进行分类或分组。最通用的分类方式是使用"飞机"这一术语。该术语在《联邦法规第14篇》（14 CFR 1.1）中有明确规定，指的是用于或在空中飞行的装置。

en: Ultralight vehicle is another general term the FAA uses. This term is defined in 14 CFR 103. As the term implies, powered ultralight vehicles must weigh less than 254 pounds empty weight and unpowered ultralight vehicles must weigh less than 155 pounds. Rules for ultralight vehicles are significantly different from rules for aircraft; ultralight vehicle certification, registration, and operation rules are also contained in 14 CFR 103.
zh: "超轻型车辆"（Ultralight vehicle）是联邦航空管理局（FAA）使用的另一个通用术语。该术语在《联邦法规》第14篇第103章（14 CFR 103）中有明确规定。顾名思义，带动力的超轻型车辆的空载重量必须低于254磅，而无动力的超轻型车辆的空载重量则必须低于155磅。针对超轻型车辆的规定与普通飞机的规定存在显著差异；这些车辆的认证、注册及操作相关规则同样收录在《联邦法规》第14篇第103章中。

en: The FAA differentiates aircraft by their characteristics and physical properties. Key groupings defined in 14 CFR 1.1 include:
zh: 美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）根据飞机的特性和物理属性对它们进行分类。在《14 CFR 1.1》中定义的主要分类包括：

en: Airplane—an engine-driven fixed-wing aircraft heavier than air, that is supported in flight by the dynamic reaction of the air against its wings.
zh: 飞机：一种由发动机驱动的固定翼飞行器，其重量大于空气；在飞行过程中，它是依靠空气对其机翼产生的动力反作用力来维持飞行的。

en: Glider—a heavier-than-air aircraft, that is supported in flight by the dynamic reaction of the air against its lifting surfaces and whose free flight does not depend principally on an engine.
zh: 滑翔机：也是一种重量大于空气的飞行器，但它依靠空气对其升力面产生的动力反作用力来维持飞行，且其自由飞行主要不依赖于发动机。

en: Lighter-than-air aircraft—an aircraft that can rise and remain suspended by using contained gas weighing less than the air that is displaced by the gas.
zh: 轻于空气的飞行器——这种飞行器能够通过使用密度小于其所排开空气的气体来上升并保持悬浮状态。

en: Airship—an engine-driven lighter-than-air aircraft that can be steered.
zh: 飞艇是一种由发动机驱动的、比空气轻的飞行器，且可以对其进行操控。

en: Balloon—a lighter-than-air aircraft that is not engine driven, and that sustains flight through the use of either gas buoyancy or an airborne heater.
zh: 气球是一种比空气轻的飞行器，它不依赖发动机驱动，而是通过气体浮力或机载加热装置来维持飞行。

en: Powered-lift—a heavier-than-air aircraft capable of vertical takeoff, vertical landing, and low speed flight that depends principally on engine-driven lift devices or engine thrust for lift during these flight regimes and on nonrotating airfoil(s) for lift during horizontal flight.
zh: 动力升力飞机（Powered-lift aircraft）是一种重于空气的飞行器，具备垂直起降和低速飞行的能力。在垂直起降阶段，这类飞机主要依靠发动机驱动的升力装置或发动机产生的推力来获得升力；而在水平飞行阶段，则依赖非旋转型的翼型（airfoil）来实现升力。

en: Powered parachute—a powered aircraft comprised of a flexible or semi-rigid wing connected to a fuselage so that the wing is not in position for flight until the aircraft is in motion. The fuselage of a powered parachute contains the aircraft engine, a seat for each occupant and is attached to the aircraft's landing gear.
zh: 动力伞（powered parachute）是一种由柔性或半刚性机翼组成的飞行器，该机翼与机身相连；只有在飞行器开始运动后，机翼才会处于适合飞行的位置。动力伞的机身内装有发动机、为每位乘员准备的座椅，并且该机身还与飞行器的起落架相连接。

en: Rocket—an aircraft propelled by ejected expanding gases generated in the engine from self-contained propellants and not dependent on the intake of outside substances. It includes any part which becomes separated during the operation.
zh: 火箭是一种由发动机内部自带的推进剂产生的膨胀气体推动的飞行器，其运行过程不依赖于外部物质的吸入。火箭包括在飞行过程中会分离出来的所有组成部分。

en: Rotorcraft—a heavier-than-air aircraft that depends principally for its support in flight on the lift generated by one or more rotors.
zh: 旋翼机是一种重于空气的飞行器，其在飞行中的主要支撑力来自于一个或多个旋翼所产生的升力。

en: Gyroplane—a rotorcraft whose rotors are not engine-driven, except for initial starting, but are made to rotate by action of the air when the rotorcraft is moving; and whose means of propulsion, consisting usually of conventional propellers, is independent of the rotor system.
zh: 旋翼机（Gyroplane）是一种旋翼飞行器，其旋翼在启动阶段由发动机驱动，但在飞行过程中是通过空气流动的作用自行旋转的；该飞行器的推进系统通常采用传统的螺旋桨，且与旋翼系统相互独立。

en: Helicopter—a rotorcraft that, for its horizontal motion, depends principally on its engine-driven rotors.
zh: 直升机是一种旋翼飞行器，其水平运动主要依赖于由发动机驱动的旋翼。

en: Weight-shift-control—a powered aircraft with a framed pivoting wing and a fuselage controllable only in pitch and roll by the pilot's ability to change the aircraft's center of gravity with respect to the wing. Flight control of the aircraft depends on the wing's ability to flexibly deform rather than the use of control surfaces.
zh: 重量转移控制（Weight-Shift Control）：这是一种动力驱动的飞行器，其机翼采用框架式结构并可实现旋转运动；同时，飞机的机身仅能在俯仰（pitch）和滚转（roll）方向上进行调节。这种调节机制依赖于飞行员通过改变飞机内部某些参数来调整飞机重心相对于机翼的位置。飞行控制依赖于机翼的灵活变形能力，而非控制面的使用。

en: Size and weight are other methods used in 14 CFR 1.1 to group aircraft:
zh: 尺寸和重量是《14 CFR 1.1》中用于对飞机进行分类的另外两种依据。

en: Large aircraft—an aircraft of more than 12,500 pounds, maximum certificated takeoff weight.
zh: 大型飞机——指最大认证起飞重量超过12,500磅的飞机。

en: Light-sport aircraft (LSA)—an aircraft, other than a helicopter or powered-lift that, since its original certification, has continued to meet the definition in 14 CFR 1.1. (LSA can include airplanes, airships, balloons, gliders, gyroplanes, powered parachutes, and weight-shift-control.)
zh: 轻型运动飞机（Light-Sport Aircraft，简称LSA）是指除直升机或动力升空装置以外的其他飞机类型。自其初次获得认证以来，这类飞机必须持续符合美国联邦法规14 CFR 1.1中的定义。轻型运动飞机的范围包括普通飞机、飞艇、热气球、滑翔机、旋翼飞行器（gyroplanes）、动力伞以及采用重量偏移控制技术（weight-shift control）的飞行器。

en: Small Aircraft—aircraft of 12,500 pounds or less, maximum certificated takeoff weight.
zh: 小型飞机：指最大认证起飞重量不超过12,500磅的飞机。

en: Pilot Certifications
zh: 飞行员证书

en: The type of intended flying influences what type of pilot's certificate is required. Eligibility, training, experience, and testing requirements differ depending on the type of certificates sought. [Figure 1-20] Each type of pilot's certificate has privileges and limitations that are inherent within the certificate itself. However, other privileges and limitations may be applicable based on the aircraft type, operation being conducted, and the type of certificate. For example, a certain certificate may have privileges and limitations under 14 CFR part 61 and part 91.
zh: 所需的飞行类型决定了需要哪种类型的飞行员证书。资格要求、培训内容、经验要求以及考试标准都会因所申请的证书类型而有所不同。[图1-20]每种类型的飞行员证书都具备其本身固有的权限和限制；不过，根据飞机的类型、执行的操作方式以及证书的类别，还可能存在其他额外的权限和限制。例如，某些证书可能同时受到《联邦法规》第14篇第61部分和第91部分的约束（即这些法规对持有该证书的飞行员的权限和行为有明确的规定）。

en: Privileges—define where and when the pilot may fly, with whom they may fly, the purpose of the flight, and the type of aircraft they are allowed to fly.
zh: 权限（Privileges）：规定了飞行员可以在哪些地区飞行、与谁一起飞行、飞行的目的以及他们被允许驾驶的飞机类型。

en: Limitations—the FAA may impose limitations on a pilot certificate if, during training or the practical test, the pilot does not demonstrate all skills necessary to exercise all privileges of a privilege level, category, class, or type rating.
zh: 限制（Limitations）：如果飞行员在培训或实际考试过程中未能展示出行使某一权限级别、类别或机型评级所需的所有技能，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）可能会对其飞行执照施加相应的限制。

en: Endorsements, a form of authorization, are written to establish that the certificate holder has received training in specific skill areas. Endorsements are written and signed by an authorized individual, usually a certificated flight instructor (CFI), and are based on aircraft classification. [Figure 1-21]
zh: 认可书是一种授权形式，其目的是证明证书持有人已在特定技能领域接受了培训。认可书由具有授权资格的人员（通常是持有飞行教员执照的CFI）撰写并签署，其内容依据飞机的分类标准来确定。[图1-21]

en: Sport Pilot
zh: 运动飞行员（Sport Pilot）

en: To become a sport pilot, the student pilot is required to have flown, at a minimum, the following hours depending upon the aircraft:
zh: 要成为一名运动飞行员，学员飞行员至少需要完成以下飞行小时数（具体飞行小时数取决于所驾驶的飞机类型）：

en: Commercial Pilot
zh: 商业飞行员

en: A commercial pilot may be compensated for flying. Training for the certificate focuses on a better understanding of aircraft systems and a higher standard of airmanship. The Commercial Pilot Certificate itself does not allow a pilot to fly in instrument meteorological conditions (IMC), and commercial pilots without an instrument rating are restricted to daytime flight within 50 NM when flying for hire.
zh: 商业飞行员在执行飞行任务时可能会获得相应的报酬。获取相关飞行执照的培训重点在于更好地理解飞机系统，并提升飞行技能的水平。然而，商业飞行员执照本身并不允许飞行员在仪表气象条件（IMC）下飞行；没有仪表飞行资格的商业飞行员在受雇执行飞行任务时，仅被限制在白天进行飞行，且飞行距离不得超过50海里（NM）。

en: A commercial airplane pilot must be able to operate a complex airplane, as a specific number of hours of complex (or turbine-powered) aircraft time are among the prerequisites, and at least a portion of the practical examination is performed in a complex aircraft. A complex aircraft must have retractable landing gear, movable flaps, and a controllable-pitch propeller. See 14 CFR part 61, section 61.31(e) for additional information. [Figure 1-23]
zh: 商业飞机的飞行员必须能够操作复杂的飞机；因为具备一定时长的复杂飞机（或涡轮动力飞机）驾驶经验是成为合格飞行员的先决条件之一，而且实际考核的至少一部分内容也是在复杂的飞机上进行的。所谓"复杂飞机"，是指那些配备了可收放起落架、可移动襟翼以及可调距螺旋桨的飞机。有关更多详细信息，请参阅《联邦航空规章》（14 CFR）第61部分第61.31(e)条。[图1-23]

en: Airline Transport Pilot
zh: 航空运输飞行员

en: The airline transport pilot (ATP) is tested to the highest level of piloting ability. The ATP certificate is a prerequisite for serving as a PIC and second in command (SIC) of scheduled airline operations. It is also a prerequisite for serving as a PIC in select charter and fractional operations. The minimum pilot experience is 1,500 hours of flight time. In addition, the pilot must be at least 23 years of age, be able to read, write, speak, and understand the English language, and be "of good moral standing."
zh: 航空运输飞行员（ATP）需要通过最高水平的飞行能力测试。持有ATP证书是担任定期航班运营中的机长（PIC）或副驾驶（SIC）的必要条件；同时，这也是在某些包机及小型航空公司运营中担任机长的前提要求。飞行员的最低飞行经验要求为1,500小时。此外，飞行员必须年满23岁，具备读、写、说英语的能力，并且品行端正。

en: A pilot may obtain an ATP certificate with restricted privileges enabling him/her to serve as an SIC in scheduled airline operations. The minimum pilot experience is reduced based upon specific academic and flight training experience. The minimum age to be eligible is 21 years. [Figure 1-24]
zh: 该人员持有ATP证书，具备有限的权限，因此可以担任预定航班运营中的SIC（Safety Inspection Commander）。根据特定的学术背景和飞行培训经历，所需的最低飞行员经验要求有所降低。符合条件的最低年龄为21岁。[图1-24]

en: Selecting a Flight School
zh: 选择飞行学校

en: Selecting a flight school is an important consideration in the flight training process. FAA-approved training centers, FAA-approved pilot schools, noncertificated flying schools, and independent flight instructors conduct flight training in the United States. All flight training is conducted under the auspices of the FAA following the regulations outlined in 14 CFR parts 142, 141, or 61. Training centers, also referred to as flight academies, operate under 14 CFR part 142 and are certificated by the FAA. Application for certification is voluntary and the training center must meet stringent requirements for personnel, equipment, maintenance, facilities, and must teach a curriculum approved by the FAA. Training centers typically utilize a number of flight simulation training devices as part of its curricula. Flight training conducted at a training center is primarily done under contract to airlines and other commercial operators in transport or turbine aircraft, however many also provide flight training for the private pilot certificate, commercial pilot certificate, instrument rating, and ATP certificate.
zh: 在选择飞行学校时，这是一个非常重要的考虑因素。在美国，获得FAA（美国联邦航空管理局）认可的培训中心、飞行员培训学校、非认证的飞行学校以及独立的飞行教练都会开展飞行训练。所有的飞行训练均遵循FAA制定的法规进行，具体相关法规见于14 CFR的第142部分、第141部分或第61部分。这些培训中心也被称为"飞行学院"，它们依据14 CFR第142部分的规定运营，并由FAA进行认证。申请认证是自愿的，但培训中心必须满足人员配备、设备设施等方面的严格要求，并且必须教授FAA批准的教学课程。通常情况下，这些培训中心会在其教学计划中包含多种飞行模拟训练设备。在培训中心进行的飞行训练主要是为航空公司及其他商业运营机构提供运输类或涡轮发动机的飞行员培训；不过，许多培训中心也提供私人飞行员执照、商用飞行员执照、仪表飞行资格认证（Instrument Rating）以及ATP飞行员认证（ATP Certificate）的相关培训服务。

en: Flight schools operating under 14 CFR part 141 are certificated by the FAA. Application for certification is voluntary and the school must meet stringent requirements for personnel, equipment, maintenance, facilities, and must teach an established curriculum, which includes a training course outline (TCO) approved by the FAA. The certificated schools may qualify for a ground school rating and a flight school rating. In addition, the school may be authorized to give its graduates practical (flight) tests and knowledge (computer administered written) tests. The FAA Pilot School Search database located at http://av-info.faa.gov/PilotSchool.asp, lists certificated ground and flight schools and the pilot training courses each school offers.
zh: 根据美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的14 CFR第141部分规定，运营飞行学校的机构必须获得FAA的认证。申请认证是自愿的，但这些学校必须满足人员、设备、维护设施等方面的严格要求，并且必须教授经FAA批准的标准化课程内容（包括培训课程大纲TCO）。通过认证的学校可以获得"地面学校"或"飞行学校"的评级。此外，这些学校还被授权对毕业生进行实际操作能力测试（飞行测试）以及理论知识测试（计算机管理的书面测试）。FAA的飞行员学校查询数据库（网址：http://av-info.faa.gov/PilotSchool.asp）列出了所有获得认证的地面学校和飞行学校，以及每所学校提供的飞行员培训课程信息。

en: Enrollment in a 14 CFR part 141 flight school ensures quality, continuity, and offers a structured approach to flight training because these facilities must document the training curriculum and have their flight courses approved by the FAA. These strictures allow 14 CFR part 141 schools to complete certificates and ratings in fewer flight hours, which can mean a savings on the cost of flight training for the student pilot. For example, the minimum requirement for a Private Pilot Certificate is 35 hours in a part 141-certificated school and 40 hours in a part 61-certificated school. (This difference may be insignificant for a Private Pilot Certificate because the national average indicates most pilots require 60 to 75 hours of flight training.)
zh: 在符合14CFR第141条规定的飞行学校就读，能够确保飞行训练的质量和连贯性，并为学员提供系统化的培训流程。因为这些学校必须记录所有的培训内容，其开设的飞行课程也需要获得美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的批准。这样的规定使得符合14CFR第141条规定的飞行学校能够在更少的飞行小时数内完成学员的培训，并颁发相应的证书和资格认证，从而为学员节省飞行训练的费用。例如，获得私人飞行员证书的最低要求是在符合14CFR第141条规定的学校完成35小时的飞行训练，在符合14CFR第61条规定的学校则需要完成40小时的飞行训练。（不过对于私人飞行员证书来说，这种差异可能并不显著，因为全国平均数据显示大多数飞行员实际上需要接受60至75小时的飞行训练。）

en: Many excellent flight schools find it impractical to qualify for the FAA part 141 certificates and are referred to as part 61 schools. 14 CFR part 61 outlines certificate and rating requirements for pilot certification through noncertificated schools and individual flight instructors. It also states what knowledge-based training must be covered and how much flight experience is required for each certificate and rating. Flight schools and flight instructors who train must adhere to the statutory requirements and train pilots to the standards found in 14 CFR part 61.
zh: 许多优秀的飞行学校认为，获得FAA Part 141证书的实际操作难度较大，因此这些学校被归类为Part 61学校。根据14 CFR Part 61的规定，非认证飞行学校及个人飞行教练也可以为飞行员颁发相关证书和评级。该法规详细说明了所需进行的知识培训内容，以及每项证书或评级所要求的飞行经验时长。所有从事飞行培训的飞行学校和飞行教练都必须遵守这些法定要求，并按照14 CFR Part 61中规定的标准来培养飞行员。

en: One advantage of flight training under 14 CFR part 61 is its flexibility. Flight lessons can be tailored to the individual student, because 14 CFR part 61 dictates the required minimum flight experience and knowledge-based training necessary to gain a specific pilot's license, but it does not stipulate how the training is to be organized. This flexibility can also be a disadvantage because a flight instructor who fails to organize the flight training can cost a student pilot time and expense through repetitious training. One way for a student pilot to avoid this problem is to ensure the flight instructor has a well-documented training syllabus.
zh: 根据《14 CFR第61部分》的规定进行飞行训练的一个优势是其灵活性。飞行课程可以根据每位学员的具体情况量身定制，因为该法规规定了获得特定飞行员执照所需的最低飞行经验以及基于知识的培训内容，但并未对培训的组织方式作出具体规定。然而，这种灵活性也可能带来负面影响：如果飞行教员未能妥善组织培训过程，可能会导致学员花费大量时间和金钱进行重复性训练。为了避免这一问题，学员应确保所聘请的飞行教员拥有详细记录的培训大纲（即教学计划）。

en: How To Find a Reputable Flight Program
zh: 如何找到一个信誉良好的飞行培训计划

metadata.section: How To Find a Reputable Flight Program

en: To obtain information about pilot training, contact the local FSDO, which maintains a current file on all schools within its district. The choice of a flight school depends on what type of certificate is sought, and whether an individual wishes to fly as a sport pilot or wishes to pursue a career as a professional pilot. Another consideration is the amount of time that can be devoted to training. Ground and flight training should be obtained as regularly and frequently as possible because this assures maximum retention of instruction and the achievement of requisite proficiency.
zh: 如需了解有关飞行员培训的信息，请联系当地的FSDO（Flight Standards Division），该机构会维护一份包含其辖区内所有飞行学校的最新清单。选择哪所飞行学校取决于您希望获得哪种类型的证书，以及您是希望作为业余飞行员进行飞行活动，还是希望从事职业飞行员的工作。另一个需要考虑的因素是您可以投入培训的时间。地面训练和飞行训练都应尽可能定期、频繁地进行，因为这有助于最大限度地巩固所学知识并达到所需的熟练程度。

metadata.section: How To Find a Reputable Flight Program

en: Do not make the determination based on financial concerns alone, because the quality of training is very important. Prior to making a final decision, visit the schools under consideration and talk with management, instructors, and students. Request a personal tour of the flight school facility.
zh: 不要仅基于财务方面的考虑来做出决定，因为培训的质量非常重要。在最终做出决定之前，请参观那些正在考虑中的学校，并与学校的管理层、教师以及学生进行交流。同时，要求安排一次对飞行学校设施的实地参观（个人导览）。

metadata.section: How To Find a Reputable Flight Program

en: Be inquisitive and proactive when searching for a flight school, do some homework, and develop a checklist of questions by talking to pilots and reading articles in flight magazines. The checklist should include questions about aircraft reliability and maintenance practices, and questions for current students such as whether or not there is a safe, clean aircraft available when they are scheduled to fly.
zh: 在寻找飞行学校时，要表现出好奇心和主动性，做好充分的准备工作。可以通过与飞行员交流以及阅读飞行杂志上的文章来制定一份问题清单。这份清单应包括关于飞机可靠性和维护流程的问题，同时也应该包含针对在校学生的具体问题（例如：在他们计划飞行时，是否能够使用到安全、干净的飞机）。

metadata.section: How To Find a Reputable Flight Program

en: Questions for the training facility should be aimed at determining if the instruction fits available personal time. What are the school's operating hours? Does the facility have dedicated classrooms available for ground training required by the FAA? Is there an area available for preflight briefings, postflight debriefings, and critiques? Are these rooms private in nature in order to provide a nonthreatening environment in which the instructor can explain the content and outcome of the flight without making the student feel self-conscious?
zh: 针对培训设施提出的问题应当旨在确定所提供的教学内容是否适合学员的个人时间安排。学校的营业时间是怎样的？该设施是否配备了FAA（美国联邦航空管理局）要求的地面训练专用教室？是否有专门用于飞行前简报、飞行后总结以及评估讨论的区域？这些房间是否为私密空间，以便营造一个无威胁的环境，在这种环境中，教练可以讲解飞行内容及结果，而不会让学员感到紧张或不自在？

metadata.section: How To Find a Reputable Flight Program

en: Examine the facility before committing to any flight training. Evaluate the answers on the checklist, and then take time to think things over before making a decision. This proactive approach to choosing a flight school will ensure a student pilot contracts with a flight school or flight instructor best suited to their individual needs.
zh: 在决定参加任何飞行训练之前，先仔细考察相关培训机构。认真评估检查清单上的各项内容，然后花时间仔细思考后再做出决定。这种积极主动的选校方式能够确保学员飞行员与最适合他们个人需求的飞行学校或飞行教练签订合同。

metadata.section: How To Find a Reputable Flight Program

en: How To Choose a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)
zh: 如何选择一位经过认证的飞行教员（CFI）

metadata.section: How To Choose a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)

en: Whether an individual chooses to train under 14 CFR part 141 or part 61, the key to an effective flight program is the quality of the ground and flight training received from the CFI. The flight instructor assumes total responsibility for training an individual to meet the standards required for certification within an ever-changing operating environment.
zh: 无论个人选择接受《联邦航空规章》（14 CFR）第141部分还是第61部分的培训，一个有效的飞行训练计划的关键在于从飞行教员（CFI）那里获得的地面训练和飞行训练的质量。飞行教员需承担全部责任，确保学员能够在不断变化的运营环境中达到认证所需的标准。

metadata.section: How To Choose a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)

en: A CFI should possess an understanding of the learning process, knowledge of the fundamentals of teaching, and the ability to communicate effectively with the student pilot. During the certification process, a flight instructor applicant is tested on the practical application of these skills in specific teaching situations. The flight instructor is crucial to the scenario-based training program endorsed by the FAA. He or she is trained to function in the learning environment as an advisor and guide for the learner. The duties, responsibilities, and authority of the CFI include the following:
zh: CFI（认证飞行教员）应当理解学习过程，掌握教学的基本原理，并具备与学员飞行员有效沟通的能力。在认证过程中，飞行教员申请人需要在特定的教学情境中实际运用这些技能。飞行教员对于FAA（美国联邦航空管理局）认可的基于情景的培训计划至关重要。他们接受过专门培训，能够在学习环境中担任学习者的顾问和指导者。飞行教员的职责、责任及权限包括以下内容：

metadata.section: How To Choose a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)

en: Orient the student to the scenario-based training system
zh: 引导学生使用基于场景的培训系统。

metadata.section: How To Choose a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)

en: Help the student become a confident planner and inflight manager of each flight and a critical evaluator of their own performance

metadata.section: How To Choose a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)

en: Should a student pilot find the selected CFI is not training in a manner conducive for learning, or the student and CFI do not have compatible schedules, the student pilot should find another CFI. Choosing the right CFI is important because the quality of instruction and the knowledge and skills acquired from their flight instructor affect a student pilot's entire flying career.
zh: 如果学生飞行员发现所选的合作飞行教练（CFI）的培训方式不利于学习，或者学生飞行员与合作飞行教练的时间安排不匹配，那么学生飞行员应该另找一位合作飞行教练。选择合适的合作飞行教练非常重要，因为他们的教学质量以及学生飞行员从他们那里学到的知识和技能，将直接影响其整个飞行生涯的发展。

metadata.section: How To Choose a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)

en: The Student Pilot
zh: 学生飞行员

en: The first step in becoming a pilot is to select a type of aircraft. FAA rules for obtaining a pilot's certificate differ depending on the type of aircraft flown. Individuals can choose among airplanes, gyroplanes, weight-shift, helicopters, powered parachutes, gliders, balloons, or airships. A pilot does not need a certificate to fly ultralight vehicles.
zh: 成为飞行员的第一步是选择一种飞机类型。根据所驾驶的飞机类型，美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）关于获取飞行员证书的规定也会有所不同。个人可以选择驾驶飞机、旋翼机、重心可调飞行器、直升机、动力滑翔伞、滑翔机、热气球或飞艇等。驾驶超轻型飞行器时，飞行员不需要持有相应的证书。

en: Basic Requirements
zh: 基本要求

en: A student pilot is one who is being trained by an instructor pilot for his or her first full certificate, and is permitted to fly alone (solo) under specific, limited circumstances. Before a student pilot may be endorsed to fly solo, that student must have a Student Pilot Certificate. There are multiple ways that an aspiring pilot can obtain their Student Pilot Certificate. The application may be processed by an FAA inspector or technician, an FAA-Designated Pilot Examiner, a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI), or an Airman Certification Representative (ACR). If the application is completed electronically, the authorized person will submit the application to the FAA's Airman Certification Branch (AFS-760) in Oklahoma City, OK, via the Integrated Airman Certification and Rating Application (IACRA). If the application is completed on paper, it must be sent to the local Flight Standards District Office (FSDO), who will forward it to AFS-760. Once the application is processed, the applicant will receive the Student Pilot Certificate by mail at the address provided on the application.
zh: 学生飞行员是指正在接受教练飞行员培训以获得其首张正式飞行执照的学员。在特定的、有限的情况下，这些学员被允许独自驾驶飞机（即进行单飞）。在获得单飞资格之前，学生飞行员必须持有相应的"学生飞行员证书"。有几种途径可以帮助有志成为飞行员的人取得该证书：申请流程可以由美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的检查员或技术人员、FAA指定的飞行考官、认证飞行教练（CFI），或是其他具备相关资质的航空人员来处理，或是认证代表（ACR）。如果申请是通过电子方式完成的，授权人员将把申请提交给位于俄克拉荷马州奥克拉荷马城的联邦航空管理局（FAA）的飞行员认证部门（AFS-760），提交途径为该部门的综合管理系统。如果该申请是通过纸质形式完成的，必须将其寄送到当地的飞行标准区办公室（FSDO），由该办公室将其转交给AFS-760部门。申请处理完成后，申请人将按照申请表上提供的地址通过邮件收到学生飞行员证书。

en: The aforementioned process will become effective on April 1, 2016. The new certificate will be printed on a plastic card, which will replace the paper certificate that was issued in the past. The plastic card certificate will not have an expiration date. Paper certificates issued prior to the new process will still expire according to the date on the certificate; however, under the new process, paper certificates cannot be renewed. Once the paper certificate expires, the Student Pilot must submit a new application under the new process.
zh: 上述流程将于2016年4月1日正式生效。新的证书将印在塑料卡上，以取代过去发放的纸质证书。这种塑料卡形式的证书没有有效期限制。在新流程实施之前发放的纸质证书仍会按照证书上标注的日期到期；不过根据新规定，纸质证书无法续期。一旦纸质证书过期，学生飞行员必须按照新流程重新提交申请。

en: Another significant change in the new process is that flight instructors will now make endorsements for solo privileges in the Student Pilot's logbook, instead of endorsing the Student Pilot Certificate.
zh: 新流程中的另一个重要变化是：飞行教员现在将在学员飞行员的日志簿中签署相关证明，以确认其具备单独飞行的资格（即"单飞特权"），而不是直接在学员飞行员证书上盖章或签署证明。

en: To be eligible for a Student Pilot Certificate, the applicant must:
zh: 要成为符合申请学生飞行员证书条件的申请人必须：

en: Be at least 16 years of age (14 years of age to pilot a glider or balloon).
zh: 年龄必须至少为16岁（驾驶滑翔机或热气球则需要14岁）。

en: Be able to read, speak, write, and understand the English language.
zh: 能够阅读、说、写以及理解英语。

en: Medical Certification Requirements
zh: 医学认证要求

en: The second step in becoming a pilot is to obtain a medical certificate (if the choice of aircraft is an airplane, helicopter, gyroplane, or an airship). (The FAA suggests the individual get a medical certificate before beginning flight training to avoid the expense of flight training that cannot be continued due to a medical condition.) Balloon or glider pilots do not need a medical certificate, but do need to write a statement certifying that no medical defect exists that would prevent them from piloting a balloon or glider. The new sport pilot category does not require a medical examination; a driver's license can be used as proof of medical competence. Applicants who fail to meet certain requirements or who have physical disabilities which might limit, but not prevent, their acting as pilots, should contact the nearest FAA office. Anyone requesting an FAA Medical Clearance, Medical Certificate, or Student Pilot Medical Certificate can electronically complete an application through the FAA's MedXPress system available at https://medxpress.faa.gov/.
zh: 成为飞行员的第二步是获取医疗证书（如果所选择的飞行器是飞机、直升机、旋翼机或飞艇的话）。美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）建议人们在开始飞行训练之前先取得医疗证书，以避免因健康问题而无法继续飞行训练所带来的费用损失。热气球或滑翔机的驾驶员不需要医疗证书，但他们需要签署一份声明，证明自己没有任何会妨碍他们驾驶热气球或滑翔机的健康缺陷。对于"新运动飞行员"这一类别来说，无需进行体检；驾驶执照可以作为具备医疗资质的证明。未能满足某些要求的申请人，或者有身体残疾但仍然能够担任飞行员的申请人，应联系最近的FAA办公室。任何需要申请FAA医疗许可、医疗证书或学生飞行员医疗证书的人，都可以通过FAA的MedXPress系统在线完成申请流程，该系统的网址为https://medxpress.faa.gov/。

en: A medical certificate is obtained by passing a physical examination administered by a doctor who is an FAA-authorized AME. There are approximately 6,000 FAA-authorized AMEs in the nation.
zh: 医疗证明是通过接受由FAA授权的航空医学检查员（AME）进行的身体检查后获得的。全国范围内大约有6,000名FAA授权的航空医学检查员。

en: To find an AME near you, go to the FAA's AME locator at www.faa.gov/pilots/amelocator/. Medical certificates are designated as first class, second class, or third class. Generally, first class is designed for the airline transport pilot; second class for the commercial pilot; and third class for the student, recreational, and private pilot.
zh: 如需寻找附近的航空医学检查员，您可以访问FAA的AME（航空医学评估员）查询页面：www.faa.gov/pilots/amelocator/。医疗证书分为三类：第一类、第二类和第三类。通常，第一类适用于航空公司运输飞行员；第二类适用于商业飞行员；第三类适用于学生飞行员、休闲飞行员以及私人飞行员。

en: A Student Pilot Certificate can be processed by an FAA inspector or technician, an FAA Designated pilot examiner (DPE), an Airman Certification Representative (ACR), or a Certified Flight Instructor (CFI). This certificate allows an individual who is being trained by a flight instructor to fly alone (solo) under specific, limited circumstances and must be carried with the student pilot while exercising solo flight privileges. The Student Pilot Certificate is only required when exercising solo flight privileges. The new plastic student certificate does not have an expiration date. For airmen who were issued a paper certificate, that certificate will remain valid until its expiration date. A paper certificate cannot be renewed. When the paper certificate expires, a new application must be completed via the IACRA system, and a new plastic certificate will be issued.
zh: 该证书必须由FAA检查员或技术人员、FAA指定的飞行员考官（DPE）、空军人员认证代表（ACR）或持证飞行教员（CFI）签发。持有此证书的学员在飞行教员的指导下接受培训后，可以在特定的、有限的条件下单独驾驶飞机（即进行单飞）。在行使单飞权利时，学员必须随身携带该证书。只有在进行单飞操作时才需要出示这份学员证书。新的塑料材质学员证书没有有效期；而对于那些已经获得纸质证书的空军人员来说，他们的证书将一直有效直至其到期日。纸质证书无法续期。一旦纸质证书过期，必须通过IACRA系统重新提交申请，然后才会颁发新的塑料材质证书。

en: Student Pilot Solo Requirements
zh: 学生飞行员单独飞行的要求

en: Once a student has accrued sufficient training and experience, a CFI can endorse the student's logbook to authorize limited solo flight in a specific type (make and model) of aircraft. A student pilot may not carry passengers, fly in furtherance of a business, or operate an aircraft outside of the various endorsements provided by the flight instructor. There is no minimum aeronautical knowledge or experience requirement for the issuance of a Student Pilot Certificate, however, the applicant must be at least 16 years of age (14 years of age for a pilot for glider or balloon), and they must be able to read, speak, write and understand the English language. There are, however, minimum aeronautical knowledge and experience requirements for student pilots to solo.
zh: 一旦学生完成了足够的培训并积累了相应的经验，飞行教员（CFI）就可以批准其飞行日志，从而允许其在特定类型（机型）的飞机上进行有限的单独飞行。作为学生飞行员，他们不得搭载乘客、从事商业飞行活动，也不得操作超出飞行教员所授权范围之外的飞机。虽然颁发学生飞行员证书没有最低的航空知识或经验要求，但申请人必须年满16岁（驾驶滑翔机或热气球的学生飞行员则需要年满14岁），并且必须具备阅读、表达和理解英语的能力。然而，对于学生飞行员来说，在单独飞行时确实存在一定的最低航空知识和经验要求。

en: Becoming a Pilot
zh: 成为飞行员

en: The course of instruction a student pilot follows depends on the type of certificate sought. It should include the ground and flight training necessary to acquire the knowledge and skills required to safely and efficiently function as a certificated pilot in the selected category and class of aircraft. The specific knowledge and skill areas for each category and class of aircraft are outlined in 14 CFR part 61. Eligibility, aeronautical knowledge, proficiency, and aeronautical requirements can be found in 14 CFR part 61.
zh: 学生飞行员所接受的培训课程取决于他们希望获得的证书类型。该培训应包括地面训练和飞行训练，这些训练旨在帮助他们掌握作为持证飞行员在所选飞机类别中安全、高效地执行任务所需的知识和技能。针对每种飞机类别的具体知识和技能要求，详见《联邦法规》（14 CFR）第61部分的规定。关于资格要求、航空知识、操作熟练度以及相关航空标准的信息，同样可以在《联邦法规》（14 CFR）第61部分中找到。

en: The CFI may also use commercial publications as a source of study materials, especially for aircraft categories where government materials are limited. A student pilot should follow the flight instructor's advice on what and when to study. Planning a definite study program is the key to efficient learning.
zh: 飞行教员（CFI）还可以使用商业出版物作为学习材料的来源，特别是对于那些政府材料有限的飞机类别。学生飞行员应遵循飞行教员
